seasonality: Strong performance in late 2025 with record revenue in September 2025 ($2M+), moderation in Q4, recovery in early 2026 with launch of Automated Redemption Manager (ARM). Revenue scales with Ethereum staking yields and stablecoin collateral utilization.
rationale: Origin Protocol demonstrates an extreme valuation discount (P/S 1.17x) relative to peers and DeFi benchmarks, despite executing a clean 100% revenue-to-buyback model. Monthly revenue has ranged $710K-$2M+ across 2025-2026, implying $8.5M-$24M annualized depending on product mix. Conservative estimate of $13.2M annualized reflects Sep 2025 peak ($2M/mo), Oct decline ($1M), Nov floor ($710K), and Feb 2026 recovery trajectory. The xOGN staking APR of 40% (max lock, ~1.4x multiplier over 1yr) represents TRUE YIELD: 100% of protocol revenue flows to buybacks on open market, distributed to xOGN stakers with ZERO token emissions. This is structurally superior to inflationary governance tokens. TVL of $71M spans OETH (liquid staking), OUSD (yield stablecoin), Super OETH (Base/Optimism LST aggregator), and primeETH. 50% of circulating supply (662M OGN) is currently staked. The extreme P/S discount likely reflects: (1) small market cap ($15.5M) with limited liquidity, (2) perception of being a 'yield infrastructure' play rather than narrative-driven governance token, (3) concentration in Ethereum ecosystem. Compared to Yearn (governance + fees), Frax (algorithmic + buyback), and Liquity (protocol revenue share), OGN's pure buyback model is uniquely clean but heavily discounted.
